title: GED HELP - 211info
text: MULTNOMAH COUNTY LIBRARY GED HELP Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers online resources, study materials, and databases to prepare for the General Educational Development (GED) test. Provides wraparound services, including one-on-one tutoring, test fees, and bus tickets. Eligibility: Age 18 and older, not currently in high school, and have not graduated from high school Hours: Varies by location Email (503) 577-9984 Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers GED Success (503) 577-9984 Main phone (503) 988-5123 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call, email, or visit website for more information FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None required at intake LANGUAGES: English, Spanish AREA SERVED: Multnomah County Services: GED/High School Equivalency Test Instruction Other Services Offered At This Location: EDUCATIONAL PROGRAMS - ADULT LITERACY TUTORING EDUCATIONAL PROGRAMS - CITIZENSHIP CLASSES EDUCATIONAL SUPPORT SERVICES - HOMEWORK CENTER INFORMATION SERVICES - EVENTS AND CLASSES INFORMATION SERVICES - MOBILE AND PARTNER LIBRARIES SOCIAL DEVELOPMENT AND ENRICHMENT - MY DISCOVERY PASS If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service.
